skill.name: kling-3-0
skill.description: Kling 3.0 video generation on RunComfy. Kling 3.0 (also called Kling V3.0) is Kuaishou Technology's third-generation multi-shot video model with native synchronized audio and consistent character identity across shots. This skill covers all six Kling 3.0 endpoints, spanning three rendering tiers (Standard, Pro, 4K) and two modes (text-to-video, image-to-video). Calls runcomfy run kling/kling-3.0/<tier>/<mode> through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "kling", "kling 3.0", "kling v3", "kling pro", "kling 4k", "kling text to video", "kling image to video", or any explicit ask to generate or animate with Kling 3.0.
skill.summary: Generate cinematic multi-shot video with Kling 3.0 on RunComfy. Covers all six endpoints across Standard, Pro, and 4K tiers.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: The skill consists of a single SKILL.md documentation file describing the Kling 3.0 video generation service on RunComfy. Static analysis flagged 99 patterns including backtick execution, hardcoded URLs, path traversal, and weak crypto - all are false positives. The 'backtick' patterns are markdown code block syntax in CLI usage examples. URLs are legitimate documentation links to runcomfy.com. Path references are documented CLI parameters (--output-dir, ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json). No actual executable code exists in the skill. The skill documents secure token storage (0600 mode) and explicit outbound endpoint whitelisting.

low_findings.title: Documentation describes CLI invocation patterns
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md contains markdown code blocks showing runcomfy CLI usage with parameters like --output-dir and --input. These are documented usage examples, not executable code. The skill itself contains no code that runs shell commands.

low_findings.title: Documentation references token storage path
low_findings.description: Line 282 documents that runcomfy login stores tokens at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. This is a security-positive disclosure of the token storage location and permissions.

content.user_title: Generate cinematic video with Kling 3.0 on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Kling 3.0 is a multi-shot video model with synchronized audio, but navigating six endpoints across three tiers is complex. This skill routes your request to the right Kling 3.0 endpoint and invokes the RunComfy CLI.
actual_capabilities: Invoke Kling 3.0 Standard, Pro, and 4K tiers through the RunComfy CLI
Generate text-to-video and image-to-video outputs with up to 15 seconds duration
Produce synchronized native audio with generate_audio flag
Preserve character identity across multi-shot prompts in a single generation
Handle source image inputs via HTTPS URL for i2v mode
limitations: Maximum 15 seconds per generation across all tiers
i2v mode requires a publicly fetchable HTTPS image URL (no local files)
Aspect ratios are fixed to six documented options
4K output files are large and require planning for disk and bandwidth

use_cases.title: Brand marketing video creation
use_cases.description: Generate cinematic brand stories and product videos with consistent characters across multiple shots using Kling V3.0 Pro tier.

use_cases.title: Social media content production
use_cases.description: Create vertical 9:16 shorts and social-first clips quickly and cheaply using Kling 3.0 Standard tier.

use_cases.title: 4K cinematic film production
use_cases.description: Produce native 3840x2160 finished masters for big-screen or high-resolution delivery using Kling V3.0 4K tier.

prompt_templates.title: Basic text-to-video generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-second 16:9 video of [describe your scene with motion and camera language] using Kling 3.0 Standard tier.

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-video animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this image: [image URL] with [describe motion]. Use Kling V3.0 Pro i2v with 8-second duration and generate_audio enabled.
prompt_templates.scenario: Animating a still image with motion

prompt_templates.title: Multi-shot cinematic story
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-shot Kling 3.0 Pro video: Shot 1: [scene]. Shot 2: [scene]. Shot 3: [scene]. Include synchronized dialogue with [describe audio].

prompt_templates.title: 4K high-resolution master
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 10-second native 4K cinematic video using Kling V3.0 4K tier with aspect_ratio 21:9 and generate_audio enabled.

output_examples.input: Generate a 5-second video of a barista pulling an espresso shot, vertical 9:16 format, standard tier.
Animate this portrait with gentle camera dolly-in, preserve identity, 4K resolution.
output: Identity preservation enabled, Audio: ambient only

output_examples.input: Create a multi-shot brand story with three scenes and synchronized dialogue, Pro tier.

best_practices: Lead prompts with motion and camera language like wide shot, tracking shot, or handheld follow
Number multi-shot prompts as Shot 1, Shot 2, Shot 3 to preserve character identity across scenes
Use Standard tier for drafts and A/B testing before committing to Pro or 4K renders
Set cfg_scale to 0.5 for natural motion, raise to 0.7-0.9 for strict prompt adherence on stylized output
anti_patterns: Requesting more than 15 seconds in a single generation call (will return 422 error)
Using conflicting style cues in one prompt - simplify to one or two style anchors
Demanding aggressive multi-shot plus 15s plus dialogue plus 6 cuts on 4K without validating with Standard first
Attempting to use local file paths for i2v image input (only HTTPS URLs are supported)

faq.question: What is the maximum duration of a Kling 3.0 clip?
faq.answer: 15 seconds per generation across all three tiers. For longer narratives, segment the script into multiple calls and stitch.

faq.question: How is Kling V3.0 4K priced compared to Standard and Pro?
faq.answer: Kling V3.0 4K is a flat $0.42 per second whether or not audio is enabled. Standard is $0.084/s without audio. Pro is $0.112/s without audio. The 4K tier costs roughly 5x Standard for the resolution upgrade.

faq.question: Does Kling 3.0 support multi-shot in a single generation?
faq.answer: Yes. All Kling 3.0 endpoints accept multi-segment prompts. Number the shots (Shot 1, Shot 2, etc.) and Kling 3.0 will preserve character identity across them.

faq.question: Can Kling 3.0 generate audio?
faq.answer: Yes. Set generate_audio to true. Kling 3.0 produces synchronized dialogue, ambient sound, and music in the same generation pass. On 4K the price stays flat at $0.42/s; on Standard and Pro the rate jumps about 50% with audio.

faq.question: What aspect ratios does Kling 3.0 support?
faq.answer: 16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:3, 3:4, and 21:9. The 4K tier renders 21:9 as wide cinema crops at native 3840x2160.

faq.question: Does Kling 3.0 i2v support a tail image for controlled endings?
faq.answer: Yes. The tail_image_url parameter locks the final frame; Kling 3.0 interpolates motion from source to tail image.
